2007 2006 2005 2004 2003 2002 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 1989 1988 1987 1986 1985 1984 Skip to sidebar Model-Driven Development - From Object-Oriented Design to Actor-Oriented Design Edward A. Lee Extended abstract o

-Oriented Design Edward A. Lee Extended abstract of an invited presentation at Workshop on Software Engineering for Embedded Systems: From Requirements to Implementation (a.k.a. The Monterey Workshop) Chicago, September 24, 2003 Prepublished version

) Chicago, September 24, 2003 Prepublished version Published version ABSTRACT Most current software engineering is deeply rooted in procedural abstractions. These say little about concurrency, temporal properties, and assumptions and guarantees in th

l properties, and assumptions and guarantees in the face of dynamic system structure. Actor-oriented design contrasts with (and complements) object-oriented design by emphasizing concurrency and communication between components. Components called act

nication between components. Components called actors execute and communicate with other actors. While interfaces in object-oriented design (methods, principally) mediate transfer of the locus of control, interfaces in actor-oriented design (which we

rol, interfaces in actor-oriented design (which we call ports) mediate communication. But the communication is not assumed to involve a transfer of control. This paper explores the use of behavioral type systems in actor-oriented design. Contact Upda
ype systems in actor-oriented design. Contact Update Help Update Copyright © 1999 - UC Regents; all rights reserved.
